topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my phone screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into the quiet hum of my evening. A profile picture loaded: a grinning, familiar face that used to radiate pure, unadulterated malice. It was Mark. My Mark. A knot tightened in my stomach, a familiar clenching sensation that started low in my abdomen and radiated outward. I stared at the request, my thumb hovering over the 'Accept' button as if it were a live grenade. Why now? After all this time, after I’d built a life entirely separate from the shadow he cast, he wanted to reconnect. I felt a distinct urge to delete the application immediately, to erase the digital tether before it could pull me back into that suffocating history.

[story 2]

I picked up my phone, the glass cool against my palm. The sight of his name made my jaw tighten. It wasn't anger, not exactly; it was more like a dull, heavy pressure behind my eyes, a slow-building irritation that made my shoulders involuntarily hunch up around my ears. I scrolled through my contacts, tracing the familiar number, and then I saw it—a pending request from Mark. A small, involuntary sigh escaped my lips, a sound that held all the weariness of years spent bracing for impact. I didn't open the message. I just set the phone face-down on the table, the faint glow mocking the sudden, unwelcome stirring of old grievances.

[story 3]

When the alert chimed, I froze mid-sip of my tea. The sender’s name flashed across the screen, instantly triggering a tense stillness in my posture. My knuckles turned white where I gripped the mug. It was him. The memory of his sneering face, the way he used to make every moment feel like a performance for his amusement, flooded back, sharp and unwelcome. A slow burn started in my chest, a simmering heat that made my breath catch. I didn't want to engage. I wanted the screen to go dark, to swallow the notification whole, leaving no trace of his presence in my space. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of the room, the movement jerky and restless.
